title: build(deps): bump github.com/containers/storage from 1.24.2 to 1.24.3
body:
Bumps [github.com/containers/storage](https://github.com/containers/storage) from 1.24.2 to 1.24.3.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/containers/storage's releases.

v1.24.3

Revert returning storageOpts early in rootless mode.
Log message when graphdriver is not set
Commits
  • d716b83 Bump to v1.24.3
  • a70c750 Merge pull request #781 from rhatdan/master
  • ff80b0b Log message when graphdriver is not set
  • 0964506 Revert returning storageOpts
  • 85e19ae Merge pull request #779 from rhatdan/version
  • 35ce794 Move to v1.24.3-dev
  • See full diff in compare view

comments.body: @cevich @edsantiago Same issue here with driver name not being set in /etc/containers/storage.conf on VMs.

> Same issue here

On the heals of getting new images into podman, I've got a PR for this here too: #2631  

Temporary workaround can go into `./contrib/cirrus/setup.sh`
Okay, I can live with this for a short while.
@cevich the question is whether it will pass or not.  Has not been passing in the past.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot-preview[bot]*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Sorry, I only just got to look at this. @rhatdan can you elaborate on the reasoning behind this? My recollection (which is from over a year ago) is that buildah tests simply don't even work using overlay; but also it doesn't matter because the tests explicitly force vfs: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/d69f76ab1741c834303b9dfc8d03813fe3a59577/tests/helpers.bash#L6

Do you have a link to a test failure log that demonstrates the problem you're trying to address? (Scanning through past cirrus logs would take me a good chunk of the afternoon)

Aha, here's an [example](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5616696671076352/logs/unit_test.log). And what I see is that the tests that are failing all seem to have `Chroot` in the name. This looks like a Big Clue. Does that offer anything for you to look into?
Are these tests happening inside of a container?  The question is where is the /etc/containers/storage.conf without the driver defined.

I would've liked an explanation of the `drivers` addition in the commit message, but won't block because of it.

/lgtm
/hold

Failure in [not ok 376 user-and-network-namespace](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5186042649116672/logs/integration_test.log):
```
# $ buildah run --cni-config-dir=/var/tmp/tmpce906c371aa43ccbe3ebfced/no-cni-configs --net=host alpine-working-container readlink /proc/self/ns/net
# net:[4026531992]
# open state file /proc/77921/stat: No such process
# kill container: No such process
# error running container: error reading container state (got output: ""): exit status 1
```
Am assuming it's a flake, and have restarted it.
